sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a history of diabetes type two, mild intermittent asthma, and right elbow pain exacerbated by tennis activity. He describes the elbow pain as sharp and stabbing, rated 8/10 at its worst, and 3/10 at rest. He reports taking metformin twice daily and albuterol as needed. He also states he has an allergy to penicillin and was treated for a gallbladder removal ten years ago. He mentions a fever recently, but denies it currently.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The right elbow exhibits moderate lateral epicondylitis tenderness to palpation. The cardiovascular exam reveals a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur. The hemoglobin A1c level is pending. The chest x-ray shows no acute fracture or effusion. The physical exam is otherwise unremarkable.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The primary problem is acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow. The patient also has type 2 diabetes and mild asthma.
medical_concepts: acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Increase metformin to 1000mg twice daily. Prescribe ultram 50mg once daily for pain. Order a hemoglobin A1c and a CBC. Provide a prescription for albuterol refill. Provide a brace for the right forearm. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to reevaluate.

medical_concepts: acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow
